finding: A signed Verification Summary Attestation should not be accepted from a bare DSSE signature or structurally valid bundle. Acceptance must also bind the exact statement digest, cryptographic verification result, transparency evidence, certificate-time validity, and an exact issuer and subject identity policy.

primary_sources.observed: Sigstore bundle format v0.3.2 packages verification material and signature content; attestation content is a DSSE envelope with payloadType application/vnd.in-toto+json and exactly one signature.
The formal bundle schema separates verification material from message-signature or DSSE content.
DSSE defines an envelope and signing protocol; application trust policy remains external to envelope structure.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical committed logic

uncertainty: The contract consumes an external verifier result and does not itself perform cryptography.
trust_limit: A passing record does not prove the verifier binary, trust root, certificate authority, identity provider, transparency service, or underlying VSA claim is truthful.
falsification_route: Verify the same bundle and statement with an independently installed Sigstore-compatible verifier and pinned trust root. Reject acceptance if statement digest, signature, transparency, certificate-time, issuer, or subject results disagree.
privacy_boundary: Public specifications, public repository paths, synthetic test identities, and commit metadata only; no credentials, private chat, health material, or secret values included.
